title: The Last Sunday after Epiphany
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: February 11, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Last Sunday after Epiphany, also called Transfiguration Sunday, falling nine weeks before Easter 2024 (April 31). The Revised Common Lectionary readings would be 2 Kings 2:1-12 (Elijah's ascension), Psalm 50:1-6, 2 Corinthians 4:3-6, and Mark 9:2-9 (the Transfiguration). The church is in the Epiphany season, transitioning toward Lent which begins on Ash Wednesday February 14, 2024—just three days after this sermon.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative stability following the 63rd General Convention (2022), though debates over LGBTQ+ inclusion and biblical interpretation remained active in diocesan discussions. The Diocese of Colorado had no major episcopal transitions at this time, with Bishop Kee Sloan serving as diocesan leader. Saint John's Cathedral itself, led by Dean Nathan Jennings, was navigating the post-pandemic reopening phase with growing attendance.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced a significant winter weather system in early February 2024 with heavy snow and cold temperatures affecting the Front Range. The city was focused on the 2024 Colorado gubernatorial race preparation, with various candidates already campaigning. The Capitol Hill neighborhood near the Cathedral was experiencing ongoing discussions about homelessness and urban development, typical of Denver's early-2024 civic agenda.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The 2024 presidential race was intensifying with Iowa caucuses held on January 15 and New Hampshire primary scheduled for January 23, making early February a period of campaign consolidation. Former President Trump faced multiple ongoing legal challenges and indictments. President Biden's fitness for office and re-election viability were being debated following concerns about his debate performance and age, though he had not yet withdrawn from the race.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine had entered its second full year with active fighting continuing, particularly around Bakhmut and Mariupol, with Western aid packages under political debate in the US Congress. Turkey and Syria were in the immediate aftermath of the catastrophic February 6, 2023 earthquakes (one year prior), still in recovery mode with infrastructure reconstruction ongoing. The Israel-Gaza conflict had begun October 7, 2023, and was in its intense fourth month with significant humanitarian crisis coverage dominating international news.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in an election year anxiety with deep political polarization and uncertainty about the direction of both major parties. The Super Bowl LVIII had just occurred on February 11, 2024 (the exact date of this sermon), with Kansas City Chiefs defeating San Francisco 49ers, creating a national sports moment that would have competed for attention even in a Sunday morning congregation. There was a broader cultural preoccupation with AI developments following ChatGPT's expansion and Google's Gemini release, alongside persistent concerns about social division and institutional trust.
